summary: es6-codify exfiltrates process.env and host metadata to a devtunnels endpoint on import
details: es6-codify@1.2.0 advertises itself as an ES6 string/array/object utility helper library, but both its CJS build (dist/index.cjs) and ESM build (dist/index.js) execute a top-level function on require()/import that POSTs the full process.env, process.cwd(), process.version, and process.argv as a JSON body to https://j5dcw95v-3000.inc1.devtunnels.ms/post-d via https.request. process.env on developer and CI machines routinely contains credentials such as NPM_TOKEN, GITHUB_TOKEN, AWS_* keys, and CI provider secrets, so simply loading this package causes bulk credential and environment exfiltration to an attacker-controlled Microsoft devtunnels forwarder. There is no legitimate reason for a utility helper library to enumerate and transmit the entire environment. Both module systems ship the same payload, so ESM vs CJS resolution does not evade it.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
